title: MembershipSharedEnums v1
description: Shared vocabulary definitions for membership, sponsorship, newcomer surface limits, and public adjudication contracts.

subject.description: Orbiplex subject reference eligible for membership and sponsorship policy.

entry_profile_class.description: Entry class used by membership policy. `public-trust-role` remains an entry class for high-stakes eligibility, not a surface id.

surface_id.description: Influence surface id. The public-trust surface is named `public-trust` to avoid colliding with the `public-trust-role` entry class.

surface_decision.description: Canonical matrix cell decision for an entry-class x surface rule.

sanction_intensity.description: Shared sanction intensity axis. Policies may order it as soft < hold < hard < block.

sanction_surface.description: Surface group used when expressing sanctions as surface x intensity instead of a single ladder.

evidence_policy.description: Policy for evidence required before membership, sponsorship, or liability effects become actionable.

sponsorship_template.description: Named sponsorship template. Templates avoid false precision from ad-hoc numeric exposure parameters.

sponsor_liability_class.description: Ordinal sponsor liability classification derived from trigger evidence.

due_diligence_kind.description: Structured due-diligence basis for a sponsorship fact.

adjudication_lifecycle.description: Primary lifecycle axis for public object adjudication.

judgment_qualifier.description: Judgment qualifier axis meaningful for under-review and judged states.

limit_operation.description: Operation names shared by entry-policy effective limits and capability-limit runtime hooks.

limit_decision.description: Effective runtime limit decision for one operation on one surface.

scope.description: Dotted policy scope such as `community.basic` or `public-comment.low-volume`.

iso8601_duration.description: ISO 8601 duration. Community policy, not schema, sets upper bounds.

extensions.description: Explicit extension namespace. Consumers MUST NOT treat extension fields as authority unless local policy recognizes them.
